channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Ukraine is reforming its recruitment efforts to attract younger soldiers and boost forces
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine is in the final stages of drafting recruitment reforms to attract 18- to 25-year-olds who are currently exempt from mobilization as it looks for ways to bolster its fighting force, the battlefield commander recently appointed to the President’s Office said.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Tennessee Republican proposes amendment to allow Trump to serve third term
item.description: Like other U.S. presidents, Trump is barred from running for a third White House term by the 22nd Amendment to the Constitution.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KRON4

item.title: Snowmen replace sandcastles on Alabama beach after historic winter storm
item.description: Many people who don't normally get to experience snow found unique ways to enjoy the rare sight this week as a historic winter storm moved across the South.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, WFLA, NBC

item.title: M23 rebels battling Congo's army close in on Goma as panic spreads among city's 2 million people
item.description: GOMA, Congo (AP) — Panic spread in eastern Congo's main city on Thursday, with M23 rebels steadily inching closer to Goma and seizing a nearby town as they battle the Congolese army.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN KABUMBA and MARK BANCHEREAU, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump signs order directing release of JFK assassination files
item.description: The order directs the director of national intelligence and attorney general to present a plan within 15 days for the “full and complete release of records relating to the assassination of President John F. Kennedy.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Resale prices for iPhones 'with TikTok installed' soar up to $50,000
item.description: TikTok is no longer available in the App Store as of Thursday, however there's still a way to get back on the platform – for a price.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, Fox 5

item.title: Florida man arrested after allegedly climbing over wall at Trump's Mar-a-Lago estate
item.description: A man allegedly climbed over the wall that surrounds President Donald Trump's Mar-a-Lago estate in Palm Beach, Florida, according to reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Southern cities get creative to clear leftover snow and ice after rare winter storm
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Days after a winter storm dropped ice and record-breaking snow, cleanup efforts were underway Thursday in several major Southern cities such as New Orleans, where crews were removing snow the same way they remove trash, drink cups and plastic beads after Mardi Gras.
item.dc:creator: JACK BROOK and RUSS BYNUM, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Police review writings tied to Nashville school shooter who killed female student and himself
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — The investigation into why a Nashville high school student fatally shot a classmate before killing himself has centered on his online writings, which authorities describe as concerning warning signs.
item.dc:creator: KIMBERLEE KRUESI and KRISTIN M. HALL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Florida officer accidentally shoots man with his own gun
item.description: A Florida police officer is without a job after accidentally discharging a man's gun during a traffic stop.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: CNN is announcing layoffs as part of a further shift to digital business
item.description: CNN announced a restructuring Thursday that includes some 200 layoffs, an accelerated pivot to digital operations and new TV roles for personalities like Wolf Blitzer, Jake Tapper and Audie Cornish.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Bourbon Street terror attack, Trump Tower Cybertruck explosion probe launched by Senate committee
item.description: The Senate Judiciary Committee is investigating the New Orleans terrorist attack that left 15 people dead, as well as the explosion outside the Trump hotel in Las Vegas.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Post Malone teams up with Oreo on limited-edition cookie flavor
item.description: Oreo has partnered with Post Malone for its newest cookie, featuring a first-of-its-kind filling for the brand.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Colorado DNA analyst appears on forgery charges as validity of more than 500 cases in doubt
item.description: GOLDEN, Colo. (AP) — A former Colorado Bureau of Investigation DNA scientist is facing 102 criminal charges, including dozens of counts of forgery, after authorities said she cut corners and didn't follow standard testing protocols in hundreds of criminal cases.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BROWN and COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump won't ban immigration arrests at churches. Now clergy are weighing how to resist
item.description: Numerous faith leaders across the U.S. say the immigration crackdown launched by President Donald Trump’s new administration has sown fear within their migrant-friendly congregations.
item.dc:creator: GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO, LUIS ANDRES HENAO and DEEPA BHARATH, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump says he may withhold federal aid for Los Angeles fires
item.description: The president repeated false claims that the state's fish conservation efforts in the northern part of the state are responsible for fire hydrants running dry in urban areas.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Trump pumps brakes on Day 1 tariffs
item.description: President Donald Trump pumped the brakes on his plan to deliver wide-ranging tariffs as soon as he took office, slow-tracking and toning down changes to the U.S. trade system that figured as a centerpiece of his campaign.
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, WFLA, NBC

item.title: As fire-fanning winds continue, Southern California readies for potential rain and toxic runoff
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Parched Southern California continued to face dangerous winds but could get some badly needed rain this weekend, forecasters said Tuesday, dampening the prospects of another round of killer wildfires though even a small amount of precipitation could could create new challenges lik
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump rolls back Biden directive to study methods of lowering prescription costs
item.description: Among the dozens of Biden-era executive orders he rescinded on day one, President Trump nixed one that aimed to explore new health care payment models that would lower prescription costs for Medicare and Medicaid beneficiaries.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, Fox 5

item.title: 11-year-old pulls ultra rare Paul Skenes rookie card, Pirates says massive trade deal stands
item.description: The card, which contains the "Debut Patch" worn by Paul Skenes, quickly became a highly-coveted item. After months of search, an 11-year-old has reportedly come up with it.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, WGN 9

item.title: The ash left behind by the Los Angeles wildfires might be toxic, experts warn
item.description: Toni Boucher threw up the first time she saw the charred remains of her home and neighborhood after this month’s deadly Los Angeles-area wildfires. Now she wonders if it’s worth it to go back to sift through the ashes and try to find her grandmother’s wedding ring.
item.dc:creator: MELINA WALLING, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump's first day back in White House includes firings, infrastructure announcement
item.description: President Donald Trump is spending his first full day back in the White House meeting with congressional leaders, making an infrastructure announcement and demonstrating one of his favored expressions of power: firing people.
item.dc:creator: Michelle L. Price, KTLA 5

item.title: First female Coast Guard commandant ousted by Trump administration with no explanation
item.description: Coast Guard Commandant Adm. Linda Fagan, the first female uniformed leader of an Armed Forces branch in U.S. history, was ousted by the Trump administration with no explanation late Monday.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, KTLA 5

item.title: Garth Hudson, The Band's last surviving member, dies at 87
item.description: Garth Hudson, the Band's virtuoso keyboardist and all-around musician who drew from a unique palette of sounds and styles to add a conversational touch to such rock standards as "Up on Cripple Creek," “The Weight” and "Rag Mama Rag," has died at age 87.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: More than 200 potential jurors summoned for trial of prosecutor in Ahmaud Arbery's death
item.description: BRUNSWICK, Ga. (AP) — More than 200 potential jurors were summoned Tuesday to a Georgia courthouse for questioning about whether they can serve impartially in the trial of a former prosecutor accused of meddling with police as they investigated the 2020 killing of Ahmaud Arbery.
item.dc:creator: RUSS BYNUM, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Nose spray to treat depression? FDA approves first-of-its-kind medicine
item.description: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration has approved a nose spray to help treat depression, Johnson & Johnson announced Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, ABC 4

item.title: Deep South hit with rare winter storm; historic snowfall expected
item.description: Millions of Americans face an artic blast, including the first-ever blizzard warning for parts of the Gulf Coast.
item.dc:creator: Xavier Walton, Fox 5

item.title: Cannon blocks DOJ from sharing Smith's Mar-a-Lago report with lawmakers
item.description: U.S. District Judge Aileen Cannon blocked the Justice Department from sharing with four top lawmakers the volume of former special counsel Jack Smith’s report reviewing his investigation into President Trump’s mishandling of classified records.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Trump attends prayer service at National Cathedral
item.description: After being sworn in on Monday, President Donald Trump marked his first full day in office with a prayer service at Washington National Cathedral on Tuesday morning.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, WGN 9

item.title: Here are the biggest fights Democrats are bracing for in Trump's second term
item.description: House Democrats are bracing for major fights in the coming year as President Trump enters the White House for his second term and Republicans control all levers of power in Washington.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, Fox 5

item.title: Ohio State wins 1st national title since 2014, outlasting Notre Dame 34-23 in CFP championship game
item.description: Instead of crying over another collapse, Ohio State can celebrate another national title after holding off a Notre Dame comeback bid Monday night to walk away with a nailbiter of a 34-23 victory over the Fighting Irish.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 250 roosters, guns, cash seized in Southern California cockfighting bust
item.description: (KTLA) - Authorities in San Bernardino County arrested two men, detained 33 others and seized more than 200 fighting roosters, U.S. currency and dozens of firearms, officials announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, KTLA 5

item.title: Democrat congressman condemns 'what appears to be a Heil Hitler salute' from Musk
item.description: Rep. Jerry Nadler (D-N.Y.) was among those who sharply criticized billionaire Elon Musk on Monday for making "what appears to be a Heil Hitler salute" during an event celebrating the inauguration of President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WGN 9

item.title: Trump rolls out his blueprint on border security, but his orders will face challenges
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump rolled out a blueprint to beef up security at the southern border in a series of executive orders that began taking effect soon after his inauguration Monday, making good on his defining political promise to crack down on immigration and marking another wild
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, GISELA SALOMON and ELLIOT SPAGAT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Authorities say a US border patrol agent was fatally shot in Vermont south of border
item.description: COVENTRY, Vt. (AP) — A U.S. Border Patrol agent was fatally shot Monday on a highway in northern Vermont south of the Canadian border, authorities said. The death was confirmed by the FBI and Benjamine Huffman, acting secretary of the Department of Homeland Security in Washington.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Bitter cold spreads across much of the US as Texas and the South brace for rare winter storm
item.description: Frigid temperatures engulfed the South on Monday ahead of a winter storm that's expected to spread heavy snow and disruptive ice around a region from Texas to north Florida that rarely sees such weather, sending residents rushing to insulate pipes, check heating systems and stock up on emergency sup
item.dc:creator: SARAH BRUMFIELD, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Do US adults support Trump's Day 1 actions? Here's what polling shows
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump 's second term began with efforts to deliver on key campaign promises, including his vows to crack down on immigration and restore “ energy dominance.
item.dc:creator: AMELIA THOMSON-DEVEAUX and LINLEY SANDERS, Associated Press, ABC 4
